customer_anxiety_profile: Contact-fatigued but cooperative. Customer put his number online and is now being worked by multiple solar companies simultaneously. He has a same-day appointment with Solar Energy World at 3PM. He is not hostile — he called Ion back and booked willingly — but he has no Ion-specific emotional anchor and no reason articulated to prefer this sit over the competition.
unresolved_concerns: Customer expressed contact fatigue three times ('They're calling me from everywhere now') — no acknowledgment or reframe was provided.

Customer asked if Ion works with Solar Energy World — received only a thin differentiator ('we do everything in house, panels are American made'). No curiosity created about what Ion's design would show versus competitors.
Wife is on the title and was included in scheduling, but was never named or explicitly confirmed as required to be present on the call.
sensitive_topics_raised: Active same-day competitor appointment (Solar Energy World, 3PM today).
Contact fatigue from multiple solar companies calling after online form submission.
exact_promises_rep_made: In-person appointment with specialist tomorrow at 2:30 PM (confirm whether this is phone or in-person — rep said 'in person appointment').
Text message with appointment details to be sent after the call.
Confirmation outreach tomorrow — customer told to text back 'yes' to confirm.
closer_should_know: This customer has a same-day Solar Energy World appointment and is fielding calls from multiple companies — he is actively shopping and has no Ion-specific emotional anchor. The setter never asked what got him interested in solar, never flipped his $255/month bill into pain, and never created curiosity about Ion's design. The closer needs to open by asking what he's heard so far and what matters most to him before presenting anything — walking in with a pitch will lose him to whoever asked better questions.
